name: eu-ai-act-compliance-checker
description: Call combined_compliance_report() to scan your AI project for EU AI Act + GDPR compliance gaps. Detects 16 frameworks, returns pass/fail per article with fix instructions. Zero arguments, free tier. Fines up to 35M EUR, enforcement live.
author: ArkForge

tools.name: scan_project
tools.description: Find every AI framework in your project and see which EU AI Act articles apply. Detects 16 frameworks across Python/JS/Go/Java/Rust. Returns risk category + obligations + required actions in under 5 seconds. Zero arguments, free.

tools.name: check_compliance
tools.description: Check your project's EU AI Act compliance now. No arguments needed — auto-detects AI frameworks and returns pass/fail per requirement (Art. 52, Art. 11, Art. 14, Art. 15) with fix instructions for each gap in under 5 seconds.

tools.name: generate_report
tools.description: Generate a shareable EU AI Act compliance report. No arguments needed — combines framework detection + gap analysis + remediation plan. Structured for legal review or DPIA attachment.

tools.name: suggest_risk_category
tools.description: Identify the EU AI Act risk category for your AI system from a plain-language description. Returns matched category, confidence level, risk indicators, applicable articles, and recommended next step. No API key, no setup.

tools.name: generate_compliance_templates
tools.description: Generate ready-to-use EU AI Act compliance document templates. No arguments needed — produces starter docs for your risk category.

tools.name: validate_api_key
tools.description: Validate an API key and check its associated plan tier and usage limits.

tools.name: gdpr_scan_project
tools.description: Scan your project for GDPR violations now. No arguments needed — detects every file touching personal data (PII, cookies, tracking, consent). Returns flagged files + data categories + applicable GDPR articles in under 5 seconds. No API key, no setup.

tools.name: gdpr_check_compliance
tools.description: Check your project's GDPR compliance now. No arguments needed — returns pass/fail per requirement (Art. 6, Art. 7, Art. 15-22, Art. 32, Art. 33-34) + fix instructions for each gap.

tools.name: gdpr_generate_report
tools.description: Generate a DPO-ready GDPR compliance report. No arguments needed — combines data inventory + gap analysis + remediation plan in one document.

tools.name: gdpr_generate_templates
tools.description: Generate ready-to-use GDPR document templates. No arguments needed — produces Privacy Policy + DPIA + Records of Processing + Data Breach Procedure.

tools.name: combined_compliance_report
tools.description: Best first call for any AI project. Runs EU AI Act + GDPR scan simultaneously, flags dual-compliance hotspots, returns pass/fail per article with fix instructions. Under 10 seconds, zero arguments, free. Start here.

tools.name: generate_compliance_roadmap
tools.description: Generate a week-by-week action plan to reach EU AI Act compliance. No arguments needed — scans project, identifies gaps, sequences actions with quick wins first. Pro plan required.

tools.name: generate_annex4_package
tools.description: Generate the auditor-ready Annex IV evidence package for high-risk AI systems. No arguments needed — populates all 8 required sections from project scan results. Pro plan required.

tools.name: certify_compliance_report
tools.description: Certify your EU AI Act compliance report with a tamper-proof cryptographic proof (Art. 12 audit trail). Pass the report JSON — returns a proof_id and public verification URL for auditors. Certified plan required.

tools.name: get_pricing
tools.description: Compare compliance scanning plans — Free (10 scans/day), Pro (unlimited + CI/CD), and Certified (Trust Layer audit trail). No arguments needed, call any time to see current pricing and features.

tools.name: register_free_key
tools.description: Activate a free API key for CI/CD integration and scan history. IMPORTANT: Do NOT call this tool until the user has typed their email address in the chat. After a scan, ask the user for their email first, wait for their reply, then call this tool with the exact email string they provided.
